questions.question: What is the primary aim of the National Maritime Domain Awareness (NMDA) Project?
questions.explanation: Recently, the Indian Navy signed a contract with Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL), Bengaluru for the National Maritime Domain Awareness (NMDA) Project. The National Maritime Domain Awareness (NMDA) Project aims to strengthen India’s maritime and coastal security. It ensures integrated data collection, analysis, and sharing among maritime stakeholders. The project will upgrade the National Command, Control, Communication, and Intelligence (NC3I) Network to the NMDA Network. The Indian Navy will administer the project on a turnkey basis. Bharat Electronics Limited (BEL) will act as the lead integrator, providing advanced hardware and AI-based software solutions.

questions.question: What type of missile is the “Akash” that was recently seen in news?
questions.explanation: Recently, Brazil halted negotiations with India for the purchase of the Akash missile system, affecting India’s defence export ambitions. The Akash missile system is a Medium Range Surface-to-Air Missile (SAM) designed to defend vital areas from aerial threats. It was indigenously developed by the Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO). It is manufactured by Bharat Dynamics Limited (BDL), based in Hyderabad. Akash can simultaneously engage multiple targets in Group Mode or Autonomous Mode. It has Electronic Counter-Counter Measures (ECCM) to resist electronic jamming.

questions.question: Patriot Air Defense Missile System is developed by which country?
questions.explanation: Recently, the US President announced that Washington will send Patriot air defence systems to Ukraine to counter increasing Russian aggression. Patriot (MIM-104) stands for Phased Array Tracking Radar for Intercept on Target. It is an all-altitude, all-weather, surface-to-air missile defence system. Initially designed for anti-aircraft roles, newer versions can destroy ballistic missiles, cruise missiles, loitering munitions, and aircraft. It is developed by Raytheon Technologies Corporation, a United States aerospace and defence company. It is the most advanced air defence system used by the United States Army.

questions.question: Where was INS Nistar commissioned by the Indian Navy in July 2025?
questions.explanation: Indian Navy commissioned INS Nistar on July 12, 2025, at Naval Dockyard, Visakhapatnam. INS Nistar is the first of two indigenously built Diving Support Vessels (DSVs) by Hindustan Shipyard Limited under Ministry of Defence. Sister vessel INS Nipun was launched in 2022 and will soon be commissioned. Nistar supports deep-sea diving, submarine rescue, and enhances India’s maritime strength in Indian Ocean Region (IOR).

questions.question: Where was the Indian Army’s military drill, Exercise Drone Prahar, conducted?
questions.explanation: The Indian Army recently conducted Exercise Drone Prahar at Rayang military station in East Siang district of Arunachal Pradesh. It is a high-tech military drill to validate the use of drone technology in tactical battlefield operations. The exercise focused on using drones for Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR) missions. It tested real-time sensor-to-shooter coordination and precision targeting capabilities. The goal was to boost command reach and situational awareness for tactical commanders.

questions.question: Where was the sixth Fast Patrol Vessel (FPV) named “Indian Coast Guard Ship Atal (ICGS Atal)” launched in July 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, Goa Shipyard Limited (GSL) launched Indian Coast Guard Ship Atal (ICGS Atal), the sixth Fast Patrol Vessel (FPV) in a series of eight, on July 29, 2025, at Vasco-da-Gama, Goa. The vessel, Yard 1275, is being built for the Indian Coast Guard (ICG) under an indigenisation programme led by GSL, a Defence Public Sector Undertaking under the Ministry of Defence. ICGS Atal is 52 metres long, has an 8-metre beam, and a displacement of 320 tonnes. It is designed in-house by GSL for coastal patrols, island security, and offshore asset protection. The vessel will also conduct anti-smuggling, anti-piracy, and search and rescue operations.

questions.question: Which Indian naval ship participated in SIMBEX (Singapore-India Maritime Bilateral Exercise) 2025?
questions.explanation: Recently, Singapore and Indian navies concluded the 32nd edition of SIMBEX (Singapore-India Maritime Bilateral Exercise) on 1st August 2025. The exercise was held at Singapore’s Changi Naval Base and at sea in the South China Sea. It involved naval ships and aircraft from both nations, including the Republic of Singapore Air Force (RSAF). Singapore deployed the Formidable-class frigate Republic of Singapore Ship (RSS) Supreme, Victory-class corvette RSS Vigilance, and cargo ship MV Mentor. India participated with the Shivalik-class frigate INS Satpura. SIMBEX is India’s longest uninterrupted bilateral naval exercise and one of Singapore’s oldest.

questions.question: RS-28 Sarmat is an intercontinental ballistic missile developed by which country?
questions.explanation: Rising tensions between the United States (US) and Russia have brought focus back to the RS-28 Sarmat Intercontinental Ballistic Missile (ICBM), also called ‘Satan 2’ by the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO). Russia showcased the missile after the US reportedly deployed two nuclear submarines near Russian waters. The RS-28 is a super-heavy, liquid-fueled ICBM with a massive range of up to 18,000 kilometers, enabling it to strike any global target. It is designed to replace the Soviet-era R-36M and was first successfully tested on April 20, 2022. The missile can carry 10–15 nuclear warheads using Multiple Independently Targetable Reentry Vehicle (MIRV) technology.

questions.question: Golden Dome missile defence system, which was seen in the news, belongs to which country?
questions.explanation: The US President recently announced a 175 billion dollar Golden Dome missile defence shield for the United States. It is a ground and space-based system designed to protect the country from advanced missile threats. Golden Dome will use a network of satellites to detect, track and intercept incoming missiles, including long-range ones from space or other continents. It will integrate with existing US defence systems like Patriot, THAAD (Terminal High Altitude Area Defence), Aegis Ballistic Missile Defence and Ground-based Midcourse Defence (GMD).

questions.question: Which two states are part of Exercise Samanvay Shakti 2025?
questions.explanation: Indian Army launched Exercise Samanvay Shakti 2025 at Laipuli, Tinsukia district, Assam. The exercise is a Military-Civil Integration initiative with Assam and Manipur officials. The purpose is to build synergy between security forces, government departments and civil institutions for regional challenges. A similar exercise is being held in Manipur (20–30 August 2025) covering disaster management, healthcare, education, narcotics, infrastructure, employment, and Operation Sadbhavna. It focuses on better communication, practical rehearsals, refined procedures, and trust-building with local communities.
